content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. It is worth the usual 100100100 points in all divisions. Here, you need to solve the problem only for the entire array.**

For an array BBB containing MMM **distinct** integers, say [B1,B2,…,BM][B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_M][B1​,B2​,…,BM​], we define f(B)f(B)f(B) to be the number of distinct integers XXX that satisfy the following conditions:


X≥1X \ge 1X≥1; and
There exists 1≤i<j≤M1 \le i < j \le M1≤i<j≤M such that it is possible to form a non-degenerate triangle with side lengths (Bi,Bj,X)(B_i, B_j, X)(Bi​,Bj​,X).

A non-degenerate triangle is one with positive area.
You are given an array AAA containing NNN distinct integers. Find f(A)f(A)f(A).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line one integer: f(A)f(A)f(A).
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The 333 valid values of XXX are 4,5,4, 5,4,5, and 666.
